jokes.text: Last week, I gave a fire safety talk, and nobody paid any attention. It's my own fault for using PowerPoint. PowerPoint is boring. People learn in lots of different ways, but experience is the best teacher.
jokes.setup: Dwight reflects on his failed fire safety presentation
jokes.punchline: Sets up his dangerous solution - experience as the best teacher
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - dismisses PowerPoint but creates elaborate dangerous scenario. Character-driven setup for the episode's main sequence.

jokes.text: Today, smoking is going to save lives.
jokes.setup: Fire safety should prevent smoking
jokes.punchline: Dwight uses smoking to create his fire drill
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic reversal in one perfect sentence. Classic Dwight twisted logic delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Fire! Oh, fire! Oh, my goodness. What's the procedure?
jokes.setup: Michael should be the calm leader in an emergency
jokes.punchline: He immediately panics and asks others what to do
jokes.explanation: Establishes Michael's incompetence in crisis. Good character beat but not exceptional writing.

jokes.text: What's the procedure, everyone? What's the procedure? Stay... calm! Everybody just... calm down!
jokes.setup: Leaders should demonstrate calm behavior
jokes.punchline: Both are frantically yelling instructions to stay calm
jokes.explanation: Classic irony - yelling 'stay calm' while panicking. Well-executed but familiar territory.

jokes.text: I forgot my purse! Leave it, woman! Things can be replaced, Phyllis! People, human lives, however...
jokes.setup: Michael tries to sound heroic about human life being precious
jokes.punchline: His dramatic speech trails off mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at gravitas undercut by his inability to complete the thought. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: My hand! That's hot! This one's hot, too. Okay, we're trapped. Everyone for himself!
jokes.setup: Michael should lead evacuation efforts
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons leadership and declares 'every man for himself'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott cowardice - abandons responsibility the moment things get difficult.

jokes.text: Stay alive, I'm getting help! Pull me up! You're too heavy. I only weigh 82 pounds.
jokes.setup: Office workers attempt dramatic ceiling escape
jokes.punchline: Angela's claim of weighing 82 pounds while being 'too heavy' to pull up
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy setup with perfect Angela character detail. The weight claim while being unable to be lifted is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Save Bandit! (Cat yowls)
jokes.setup: Office emergency evacuation
jokes.punchline: Angela throws her cat up into the ceiling tiles during evacuation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office moment. Perfect Angela character integration - prioritizing cat over human safety in most ridiculous way possible. Became cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: The fire's shooting at us!
jokes.setup: Firecrackers popping during fake fire drill
jokes.punchline: Angela interprets the sounds as the fire attacking them
jokes.explanation: Absurd personification of fire as an active threat. Good escalation of chaos.

jokes.text: Stanley! Barack is President! You are black, Stanley!
jokes.setup: Stanley appears to be having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Michael's attempts at medical aid involve stating obvious facts about Stanley's race
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael Scott cringe - completely inappropriate response to medical emergency. Perfectly captures his misguided attempts to connect.

jokes.text: A lot of ideas were not appreciated in their time. Electricity. Shampoo.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending his fire drill idea
jokes.punchline: Compares it to electricity and shampoo as misunderstood innovations
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight false equivalency. Shampoo as a misunderstood innovation is particularly absurd.

jokes.text: When no one headed... 'Take headed of.' No one would take headed of my instructions. So you... 'Heed.' 'Heed.' 'Take heed of.' 'Take heed of.'
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to use proper grammar in formal setting
jokes.punchline: Gets completely lost in conjugating 'heed' and creates nonsense phrases
jokes.explanation: Brilliant showcase of Dwight's pseudo-intelligence. His desperate attempts at formal language create comedy gold.

jokes.text: Can you shove down? Shove down, please.
jokes.setup: Michael whispering during serious corporate meeting
jokes.punchline: Mishears 'scoot down' as 'shove down'
jokes.explanation: Minor malapropism typical of Michael's character. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: A mars life is in my hands.
jokes.setup: Michael expressing concern about employee safety
jokes.punchline: Says 'mars life' instead of 'man's life'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation. Shows Michael's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Nobody should have to go to work thinking, 'Oh, this is the place that I might die today.' That's what a hospital's for. An office is for not dying.
jokes.setup: Michael philosophizing about workplace safety
jokes.punchline: Defines hospitals as places to die and offices as places not to die
jokes.explanation: Good Michael logic - oversimplified but weirdly profound. The hospital observation is unexpectedly dark.

jokes.text: An office is a place where dreams come true.
jokes.setup: Michael's inspirational speech about offices
jokes.punchline: Claims the mundane Dunder Mifflin office is where dreams come true
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion. The contrast between his grandiose claim and the reality of paper sales is perfect.

jokes.text: Boy, have you lost your mind? Because I'll help you find it! Did I stutter?
jokes.setup: Montage of Stanley's past aggressive moments
jokes.punchline: Shows Stanley's intimidating side before revealing his vulnerability
jokes.explanation: 'Did I stutter?' became iconic Stanley quote. Perfect juxtaposition with his current vulnerable state.

jokes.text: A throne for your highness. (Andy in British accent)
jokes.setup: Andy bringing wheelchair for Stanley
jokes.punchline: Delivers it with exaggerated British accent and royal treatment
jokes.explanation: Basic Andy try-hard behavior. Character consistent but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: That could be a little confusing, because in sales ABC means 'Always Be Closing.'
jokes.setup: CPR instructor explains ABC (Airway, Breathing, Circulation)
jokes.punchline: Jim points out conflicting ABC acronym from sales
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor. Good Jim-style joke that's clever without being showy.

jokes.text: I knew exactly what to do. But in a much more real sense, I had no idea what to do.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he knew what to do in Stanley's emergency
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with 'but in a much more real sense'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-contradiction structure. 'In a much more real sense' is perfect Michael pseudo-intellectual phrasing. Became widely quoted.

jokes.text: We are not always going to be there to coddle your heart back when it disappears to be working.
jokes.setup: Michael giving motivational speech to Stanley
jokes.punchline: Mangled sentence structure makes no grammatical sense
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael sentence mangling. 'Disappears to be working' is wonderfully nonsensical.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm working in my own casket.
jokes.setup: Stanley reflecting on his work situation after heart attack
jokes.punchline: Compares office to his own casket
jokes.explanation: Darkly poetic and perfectly captures Stanley's resignation. Brilliant metaphor that's both funny and genuinely sad.

jokes.text: If we come across somebody with no arms or legs, do we bother resuscitating them? I mean, what kind of quality of life do we have there?
jokes.setup: CPR training on armless/legless dummy
jokes.punchline: Michael questions whether disabled people deserve to live
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness. Perfectly captures his ability to say exactly the wrong thing in any situation.

jokes.text: No arms or legs is basically how you exist right now, Kevin. You don't do anything.
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate disability comments
jokes.punchline: Directly applies the insult to Kevin
jokes.explanation: Brutal roast delivered with Michael's characteristic lack of filter. The directness makes it funnier and more horrible.

jokes.text: How many is that per hour? I will divide and then count to it.
jokes.setup: 100 beats per minute CPR rate
jokes.punchline: Kevin wants to convert to hourly rate and count to 6000
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - completely missing the point of urgency in CPR. His mathematical approach to life-saving is absurdly literal.

jokes.text: First I was afraid, I was petrified (to Staying Alive tune)
jokes.setup: CPR instructor suggests Staying Alive by Bee Gees
jokes.punchline: Kelly sings I Will Survive by Gloria Gaynor instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant confusion of similar disco songs. Perfect Kelly character moment - confident but wrong.

jokes.text: That's how I know you. (CPR instructor to Creed)
jokes.setup: Creed singing Staying Alive enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: CPR instructor recognizes him from 'the parking lot earlier'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly mysterious Creed moment. Implies strange pre-episode encounter while maintaining his enigmatic character.

jokes.text: Check for an organ donor card. If he has one, we only have minutes to harvest.
jokes.setup: Dummy 'dies' during CPR training
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately pivots to organ harvesting
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight pragmatism. His immediate shift from life-saving to organ harvesting is perfectly in character and darkly funny.

jokes.text: Clarice. (Dwight wearing dummy face mask)
jokes.setup: Dwight cuts face off CPR dummy for 'organ harvesting'
jokes.punchline: Wears it like Hannibal Lecter and references Silence of the Lambs
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office visual. Perfect escalation of Dwight's organ harvesting obsession. The Hannibal Lecter reference is perfectly executed horror comedy.

jokes.text: I didn't think it was very realistic in the movie, and it turns out it's pretty realistic.
jokes.setup: Asked why he cut the face off the dummy
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact assessment of Silence of the Lambs realism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that doubles down on the horror. Dwight's scientific approach to movie special effects is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: And now Dwight knows not to cut the face off of a real person.
jokes.setup: David criticizing the expensive CPR training disaster
jokes.punchline: Michael finds silver lining in Dwight learning not to mutilate humans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - finding the educational value in preventing future face-removal. The casualness of 'real person' is brilliant.

jokes.text: We don't normally download films illegally because we're honest, hard-working people. And we don't know how.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why they don't pirate movies
jokes.punchline: Admits they just don't know how rather than moral reasons
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke. Sets up moral high ground then undercuts with honest admission of technical incompetence.

jokes.text: Jim and Pam are like movie geniuses. They're catching things that are totally going over my head.
jokes.setup: Andy watching movie with Jim and Pam
jokes.punchline: They're actually discussing Pam's parents' marriage problems, not the movie
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony. Andy's admiration for their 'film analysis' when they're dealing with real relationship crisis.

jokes.text: I state my regret.
jokes.setup: Dwight forced to give formal apology
jokes.punchline: Delivers minimal, emotionless statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect malicious compliance. Dwight technically fulfills requirement while showing zero actual remorse. Economy of language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You couldn't have memorized that? I could not because I do not feel it.
jokes.setup: Michael questions why Dwight read his brief apology
jokes.punchline: Dwight honestly admits he feels no regret
jokes.explanation: Brilliant honesty from Dwight. His refusal to fake emotion even for his own benefit is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I filled him full of butter and sugar for 50 years and forced him not to exercise.
jokes.setup: Accused of almost killing Stanley
jokes.punchline: Dwight sarcastically takes credit for Stanley's lifetime of unhealthy habits
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical argument wrapped in sarcasm. Dwight makes valid point about personal responsibility in typically blunt fashion.

jokes.text: Lily was supposed to be Nicole Kidman. And it was going to be Sophie's mom, not grandmother. But then Nicole Kidman dropped out, so they went with Cloris Leachman, with a small rewrite.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining the fake movie's production history
jokes.punchline: Detailed backstory for non-existent film including Nicole Kidman casting
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about The Office creating fake movie content. Andy's film knowledge applied to nonexistent movie is perfectly obsessive.

jokes.text: We mostly just talked about cereal.
jokes.setup: Pam asking if her dad opened up about marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact admission they discussed cereal instead
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment. Jim's honesty about meaningless conversation contrasts with episode's emotional weight.

jokes.text: If you're wearing a dress, please keep your knees together, nobody wants to see that.
jokes.setup: Michael leading meditation session
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about women's modesty during spiritual moment
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate comment. Character consistent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That one makes me think of death. It's kind of nice.
jokes.setup: Playing peaceful meditation sounds
jokes.punchline: Creed finds death-inducing music pleasant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment. His casual comfort with death imagery is disturbing and funny in equal measure.

jokes.text: And they give you a funny cigarette and you feel even more relaxed, and then you want ice cream.
jokes.setup: Michael's guided meditation about walking to a castle
jokes.punchline: Meditation scenario devolves into drug use and munchies
jokes.explanation: Michael's meditation becomes thinly veiled drug fantasy. Good absurdist escalation from spiritual to recreational.

jokes.text: Racism is dead, Stanley. You can have any kind of ice cream you want.
jokes.setup: Stanley asks for chocolate ice cream during visualization
jokes.punchline: Michael interprets this racially and declares racism over
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael racial insensitivity. His ability to make everything about race while claiming to solve racism is perfectly cringeworthy.

jokes.text: That's what he said. Right, guys? Because of gay.
jokes.setup: Oscar asked to touch Stanley's stress device
jokes.punchline: Michael forces 'that's what she said' joke and adds homophobic explanation
jokes.explanation: Forced callback that doesn't quite work. Michael's homophobic explanation makes it more cringey than funny.

jokes.text: So it wasn't Dwight after all. Looks like I'm the killer. You never expect that you're the killer. It's a great twist. Great twist.
jokes.setup: Realizing he's what's stressing everyone out
jokes.punchline: Frames his self-awareness as a murder mystery plot twist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael moment of self-awareness filtered through entertainment tropes. His ability to narrativize his own flaws is perfect.

jokes.text: 50 percent of marriages end in divorce, so it was her parents or my parents.
jokes.setup: Jim feels guilty about Pam's parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Statistical inevitability approach to relationship failure
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny rationalization. Jim's attempt to apply statistics to guilt is both logical and emotionally tone-deaf.

jokes.text: Look at that perm. That perm is so boss. It's what made me want to become a boss.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the word 'boss' used to mean cool
jokes.punchline: Reveals he became a manager because of 80s slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael origin story. His career path determined by misunderstanding slang is brilliantly absurd and character-defining.

jokes.text: Who here has the Comedy Central Roast Channel? Everybody gets together and you start hurling insults at the one guy, and everybody's laughing and everybody is hugging each other.
jokes.setup: Michael proposing a roast to reduce stress
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands that roasts end with reconciliation, not realizing his will be genuinely mean
jokes.explanation: Good setup for roast sequence. Michael's naive expectation of love sets up his eventual devastation.

jokes.text: I consider myself a good person, but I'm gonna try to make him cry.
jokes.setup: Angela's typically judgmental moral stance
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons morality for chance to hurt Michael
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela moment. Her self-righteousness immediately crumbling reveals her true vindictive nature.

jokes.text: Lower the mike for the midget.
jokes.setup: Michael hosting roast
jokes.punchline: Opens with offensive comment about short person at microphone
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate comment. Character consistent but relies on offensive language for humor.

jokes.text: If you ever put sun block on a window, you might be Michael Scott.
jokes.setup: Parody of 'You might be a redneck' format
jokes.punchline: Absurd example of Michael's stupidity
jokes.explanation: Good format parody with perfectly absurd Michael behavior. Sunblock on window is wonderfully illogical.

jokes.text: A turtle, a fridge, anybody from the warehouse. A wood chipper, Kevin, a candle and Lord Voldemort.
jokes.setup: Kelly lists things she'd rather make out with than Michael
jokes.punchline: Escalating list including Kevin and Voldemort
jokes.explanation: Good escalating insult. Including Kevin in list of undesirable makeout partners adds workplace cruelty.

jokes.text: Michael, you ran over me with your car.
jokes.setup: Meredith's turn at roast
jokes.punchline: Simply states Michael literally hit her with car
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback delivered with deadpan simplicity. No embellishment needed - the fact speaks for itself.

jokes.text: You pathetic short little man. You don't have any friends or any family or any land!
jokes.setup: Michael finally snaps at Dwight's interruption
jokes.punchline: Brutal personal attack ending with 'or any land'
jokes.explanation: Shocking Michael cruelty. 'Or any land' is unexpectedly specific insult that became widely quoted. Shows his capacity for real meanness.

jokes.text: He's supporting about 20 Nigerian princesses.
jokes.setup: Internet scam reference
jokes.punchline: Michael falls for multiple Nigerian prince emails
jokes.explanation: Good Michael gullibility joke. Multiple Nigerian princesses implies repeated falling for same scam.

jokes.text: If it were an iPod, it would be a shuffle.
jokes.setup: Kelly commenting on Michael's anatomy
jokes.punchline: Compares size to smallest iPod model
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern reference for size comparison. iPod Shuffle being the smallest makes this perfectly crafted insult.

jokes.text: What's his name? I'm thinking Roy. Roy left years ago.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming office workers are family
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know Darryl's name, guesses wrong person who left years ago
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Michael's 'family' speech. Not knowing Darryl but remembering Roy exposes his selective attention.

jokes.text: Stanley tried to die just to get away
jokes.setup: Andy's roast song about Michael
jokes.punchline: Claims Stanley's heart attack was intentional escape attempt
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny callback. Framing heart attack as suicide attempt to escape Michael is brutal but hilarious.

jokes.text: My thing isn't tiny, it's average, so get your facts straight.
jokes.setup: Michael responding to roast insults
jokes.punchline: Defensive correction about his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael defensiveness. Missing point of roast to correct factual details shows his wounded ego.

jokes.text: My guess, he's either deeply depressed or an icicle has snapped off his roof and impaled his brain. He has this terrible habit of standing directly underneath them and staring up at them.
jokes.setup: Michael doesn't come to work after roast
jokes.punchline: Dwight's oddly specific icicle death scenario with detailed explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight concern. His detailed icicle death theory shows both caring and morbid practicality. Michael's icicle-staring habit is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Michael is so dumb that he tries to put his M&M's in alphabetical order.
jokes.setup: Kevin continuing Michael jokes
jokes.punchline: Impossible task of alphabetizing identical candies
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist joke. M&Ms being identical makes alphabetizing impossible, perfect for Michael stupidity joke.

jokes.text: But he feels sorry for me because he has an incredibly powerful microscope and he can see my face.
jokes.setup: Michael's philosophical reflection on distant spaceman perspective
jokes.punchline: Even cosmic observer pities Michael when seeing him up close
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael self-awareness disguised as philosophy. The cosmic perspective collapse into personal misery is perfectly constructed.

jokes.text: When you're a kid, you assume your parents are soul mates. My kids are gonna be right about that.
jokes.setup: Pam reflecting on her parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Confident assertion about her own relationship being different
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that's also subtly funny. Pam's confidence in Jim relationship provides hope amid her parents' failure.

jokes.text: These muffins taste bad. Or an art critic. That painting is bad.
jokes.setup: Andy realizing he's not insightful enough for movie criticism
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates food and art criticism with simple 'bad' judgments
jokes.explanation: Good Andy self-awareness. His simplified critical approach shows his limitations while being endearingly honest.

jokes.text: Jim, you're 6'11" and you weigh 90 pounds. Gumby has a better body than you. Boom. Roasted.
jokes.setup: Michael's revenge roast of his employees
jokes.punchline: Wildly inaccurate height/weight with Gumby comparison
jokes.explanation: Great Michael comeback moment. 'Boom. Roasted.' became iconic catchphrase. Gumby comparison is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Pam, you failed art school. Boom. Roasted.
jokes.setup: Michael's roast continues
jokes.punchline: Hits Pam's sensitive spot about art school
jokes.explanation: Effective because it's actually true and personal. Michael's ability to find real vulnerabilities makes this cutting.

jokes.text: Kevin, I can't decide between a fat joke and a dumb joke. Boom. Roasted.
jokes.setup: Michael roasting Kevin
jokes.punchline: Makes both jokes by acknowledging the choice between them
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed meta-joke. By stating the dilemma, Michael makes both insults while appearing considerate. Peak writing efficiency.

jokes.text: Angela, where's Angela? Well, there you are. I didn't see you behind that grain of rice. Boom. Roasted.
jokes.setup: Michael looking for Angela to roast
jokes.punchline: Claims he couldn't see her behind a grain of rice
jokes.explanation: Excellent size insult with perfect delivery. The grain of rice comparison is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Stanley, you crush your wife during sex and your heart sucks. Boom. Roasted.
jokes.setup: Michael roasting Stanley
jokes.punchline: Combines weight joke with heart attack reference
jokes.explanation: Appropriately inappropriate Michael content. References recent trauma while maintaining roast format.

jokes.text: Andy, Cornell called, they think you suck and you're gayer than Oscar. Boom. Roasted.
jokes.setup: Michael roasting Andy
jokes.punchline: Fake Cornell call plus comparative homophobic insult
jokes.explanation: Hits Andy's Cornell obsession effectively. Comparative gay joke is typical Michael inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Stanley, you can throw away those pills. You are cured. Actually, better hold on to the pills, just in case.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming laughter cures Stanley's heart problems
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracks with practical medical advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - grandiose claim immediately undercut by practical concern. Shows his caring side through the pompousness.
